questions.question: Define line of sight, angle of elevation and angle of depression.
questions.answer: The line of sight is the straight line drawn from the eye of an observer to the object being viewed. The angle of elevation is the angle formed by the line of sight with the horizontal when the object is above the observer’s eye level. The angle of depression is the angle formed by the line of sight with the horizontal when the object is below the observer’s eye level.

questions.question: Explain how trigonometry helps in finding heights and distances.
questions.answer: Trigonometry helps in finding heights and distances that cannot be measured directly. By forming right-angled triangles and using trigonometric ratios, unknown heights and distances can be calculated accurately.

questions.question: Explain the role of the horizontal line in trigonometric problems.
questions.answer: The horizontal line represents the observer’s eye level and acts as a reference line for measuring angles of elevation and depression.

questions.question: What is angle of elevation? Explain with an example.
questions.answer: Angle of elevation is the angle formed by the line of sight with the horizontal when the object is above eye level. For example, looking at the top of a tower from the ground.

questions.question: What is angle of depression? Explain with an example.
questions.answer: Angle of depression is the angle formed by the line of sight with the horizontal when the object is below eye level. For example, looking down at an object from a building.

questions.question: Explain why right-angled triangles are used in trigonometry.
questions.answer: Trigonometric ratios are defined only for right-angled triangles, making them essential in trigonometric calculations.

questions.question: Explain tangent ratio with formula.
questions.answer: Tangent ratio is defined as the ratio of the opposite side to the adjacent side in a right-angled triangle. tan θ = Opposite/Adjacent.

questions.question: Explain sine ratio with formula.
questions.answer: Sine ratio is the ratio of the opposite side to the hypotenuse. sin θ = Opposite/Hypotenuse.

questions.question: Explain cosine ratio with formula.
questions.answer: Cosine ratio is the ratio of the adjacent side to the hypotenuse. cos θ = Adjacent/Hypotenuse.

questions.question: State the values of sin 30°, sin 45° and sin 60°.
State the values of cos 30°, cos 45° and cos 60°.
State the values of tan 30°, tan 45° and tan 60°.
Explain the concept of eye level in trigonometry.
questions.answer: Eye level is the horizontal line passing through the observer’s eye, used as a reference for measuring angles.

questions.question: Explain the importance of tangent ratio in real life.
questions.answer: Tangent ratio helps in finding heights and slopes in construction and surveying.

questions.question: Why is angle of elevation always acute?
questions.answer: Because the object is always above the horizontal level of the observer.

questions.question: Explain why angle of depression is equal to angle of elevation in some cases.
questions.answer: They are equal because they form alternate interior angles between parallel horizontal lines.

questions.question: Explain why tan 90° is undefined.
questions.answer: tan 90° is undefined because the adjacent side becomes zero, leading to division by zero.

questions.question: Explain one real-life application of trigonometry.
questions.answer: Trigonometry is used in surveying to measure heights and distances of inaccessible objects.

questions.question: Why is the ground assumed to be level in problems?
questions.answer: Assuming the ground is level helps in forming right-angled triangles for calculations.

questions.question: Explain the meaning of slope using trigonometry.
questions.answer: Slope is defined as the ratio of vertical rise to horizontal distance, represented by tangent ratio.

questions.question: Explain why sin 0° = 0.
questions.answer: At 0°, the opposite side is zero, so sin 0° = 0.

questions.question: Explain why cos 0° = 1.
questions.answer: At 0°, the adjacent side is equal to the hypotenuse, so cos 0° = 1.

questions.question: Explain why cosec 0° is undefined.
questions.answer: cosec 0° is undefined because sin 0° = 0 and division by zero is not possible.

questions.question: Explain the importance of trigonometric ratios.
questions.answer: Trigonometric ratios help relate angles with sides of a triangle, enabling calculation of unknown values.

questions.question: State two fields where trigonometry is applied.
questions.answer: Trigonometry is applied in construction and astronomy.
